title: Missing girl of 11: Six questions on possible failings of the French justice system
subhead: Jérôme B. is suspected of having abducted Lyhanna, who has been missing for a week. Did the justice system overlook past accusations against him? Did the courts' backlog lead to potential delays in the investigations?

Ever-extreme Eric Zemmour prepares second bid for French president
subhead: For a time, Zemmour's partner, Sarah Knafo, had positioned herself as a potential candidate for the couple's party, Reconquête. But the far-right polemicist is claiming the limelight again.

title: Seine reopens to Paris swimmers for second summer
subhead: In Paris, the swimming season was to open at three official bathing sites on July 4, the mayor's office said.

title: French Open 2026: Zverev powers past Mensik to move within one win of elusive first Grand Slam title
subhead: Alexander Zverev reached the fourth major final of his career after beating 20-year-old Czech Jakub Mensik 7-5, 6-2, 3-6, 6-3 in the Roland Garros semifinals on Friday.

title: Marjane Satrapi, powerful and outspoken artist who swore to 'stay true to herself'
subhead: Famous for her four-part graphic novel 'Persepolis' recounting her childhood in Iran during the Islamic Revolution, which she also adapted for the screen, Satrapi was also later involved with the Woman, Life, Freedom movement.

HSBC charged for helping former Lebanese central bank head embezzle hundreds of millions
subhead: The Swiss subsidiary of HSBC is suspected of having helped former Lebanese central bank chief Riad Salameh embezzle more than $300 million from the country's central bank. The charges mark a significant development in the French judicial investigation.

title: Bayeux Tapestry's transport to British Museum will be safe, detailed study determines
subhead: Before the fragile 70-meter-long embroidery is transported to the United Kingdom in July, a new risk assessment study released on Wednesday has given the move the green light.

title: Marjane Satrapi: 'At age 10, I was training myself to become a political prisoner'
subhead: The Franco-Iranian artist, who gained worldwide recognition with the graphic novel 'Persepolis,' died at 56 years old, her family announced Thursday. Le Monde interviewed her in October 2020. We are republishing that interview today.
